'''Younes Khattabi''' (born 28 March 1984)<ref name=RLWC2013>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/player/younes-khattabi/267 |title=Younes Khattabi |website=RLWC2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131103051520/http://www.rlwc2013.com/player/younes-khattabi/267 |archive-date=3 November 2013}}</ref> is a Moroccan former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}} for [[US Entraigues XIII]] in the [[Elite 2 (rugby league)|Elite 2]]. He previously played for [[SO Avignon]] and the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Khattabi was born in [[Rabat]], [[Morocco]].<ref name=RLWC2013/>

==Career==
===Club===
In the [[2007 Challenge Cup Final]] against St. Helens, Khattabi scored the first try for the [[Catalans Dragons]] thus becoming the first [[Muslim]] to score a try at [[Wembley Stadium]]<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2007/aug/27/rugbyleague.sport |title=Showpiece sacrificed to success at Saints gear up for the double |work=The Guardian |date=27 August 2007}}</ref> and the first Frenchman to score a try in a Challenge Cup final.<ref name=president>{{cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/younes-khattabi-nouveau-president-avignon/ |title=Younes Khattabi new president of SO Avignon |website=Treize Mondial |date=8 July 2022 |access-date=5 June 2025}}</ref> 

In 2018, Khattabi joined [[US Entraigues XIII]]. He made 15 appearances for the club<ref name=BackinElite2>{{cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/younes-khattabi-de-retour-en-elite-2/ |title=Younes Khattabi back in Elite 2 |website=Treize Mondial |date=29 September 2021 |access-date=5 June 2025}}</ref> before re-joining [[SO Avignon]] during the 2019–20 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/younes-khattabi-de-retour-en-elite-1/ |title=Younes Khattabi back in Elite 1 |website=Treize Mondial |date=16 January 2020 |access-date=5 June 2025}}</ref> He returned to US Entraigues the following season.<ref name=BackinElite2/>

In July 2022, Khattabi was elected as the president of SO Avignon.<ref name=president/> In January 2023, having retired from the running game, he announced his intention to play for the club's [[wheelchair rugby league]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://treizemondial.fr/younes-khattabi-va-reprendre-la-competition-en-xiii-fauteuil/ |title=Younès Khattabi will return to compete in the XIII Wheelchair |website=Treize Mondial |date=5 January 2023 |access-date=5 June 2025}}</ref>

===International===
In 2003, Khattabi represented [[Morocco national rugby league team|Morocco]] in the [[Mediterranean Cup]] scoring twice in their 58–4 win over [[Serbia national rugby league team|Serbia]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/224/serbia-4-58-morocco |title=Serbia 4 Morocco |website=European Rugby League |access-date=14 July 2024}}</ref> In 2009, he scored a try in a [[RLEF Euro Med Challenge]] match against [[Catalonia national rugby league team|Catalonia]] which Morocco won 29–6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/146 |title=Catalonia 6 29 Morocco |website=European Rugby League |access-date=14 July 2024}}</ref>

In June 2008, Khattabi represented [[France national rugby league team|France]] in a match against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://europeanrugbyleague.com/fixtures/91/france-8-56-england |title=France 8 56 England |website=European Rugby League |access-date=16 July 2024}}</ref> He played again for France at the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]] in the quarter-final match against England.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc2013.com/matchreport/9653 |title=SAT 16TH NOV 2013, 20:00 - QUARTER FINALS  |website=RLWC2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131118060052/http://www.rlwc2013.com/matchreport/9653 |archive-date=18 November 2013}}</ref>
